__fixture_tag__: VENDORED SOURCE SNAPSHOT — frozen citation, not live data

claim.property: cellulose crystallinity index (CrI) — Segal XRD method
claim.condition: XRD CuKα; Segal CrI = (I_002 − I_am) / I_002 × 100; native cellulose I; 25 C

source.primary: Segal L., Creely J.J., Martin A.E., Conrad C.M. (1959) — 'An Empirical Method for Estimating the Degree of Crystallinity of Native Cellulose Using the X-Ray Diffractometer', Textile Res. J. 29 (10), 786-794
source.standard: TAPPI T 271 — Crystallinity index by XRD
source.citation: TAPPI T 271 + Segal 1959 — kraft cellulose CrI 60-80 % (wood pulp 60-70 %, cotton ~ 80 %)

tolerance.basis: Segal method spread (intra-sample 5 %, inter-lab 10 %); spec range must overlap published 60-80 % kraft band within ± 10 % on each endpoint
